articles.summary: Residents say Israeli strikes targetted families seeking refuge in Lebanon Residents and rescue workers in southern Lebanon described scenes of devastation after Israeli bombardment struck residential areas, killing and wounding civilians displaced by earlier fighting. articles.title: Israeli lawmaker pushes bill to scrap Oslo Accords entirely
articles.summary: Israeli lawmaker pushes bill to scrap Oslo Accords entirely Far-right Israeli lawmaker Limor Son Har-Melech said a bill she is sponsoring to scrap the Oslo Accords would be reviewed on Sunday by Israel’s Ministerial Committee for Legislation. In a post on X, Son Har-Melech described the proposal as a “first and necessary” step towards preventing the establishment of a Palestinian state. articles.title: Putin says Moscow prepared to store Iranian enriched uranium
articles.summary: Putin says Moscow prepared to store Iranian enriched uranium Russian President Vladimir Putin said Moscow is prepared to transport and store Iran’s stockpile of enriched uranium, reviving an arrangement previously used under the 2015 nuclear agreement. Speaking at a news conference in Moscow, Putin said Russia had removed enriched uranium from Iran in 2015 and was ready “to repeat this experience”. articles.title: Young Palestinian killed in stabbing attack near Jerusalem checkpoint
articles.summary: Young Palestinian killed in stabbing attack near Jerusalem checkpoint A 22-year-old Palestinian man from East Jerusalem was fatally stabbed in the occupied West Bank, according to Israeli and Palestinian emergency services. Paramedics from the Palestine Red Crescent Society transported the wounded man to the al-Za’im checkpoint outside Jerusalem, where teams from Magen David Adom took him to the hospital while attempting resuscitation. articles.title: Russian president warns world will suffer if Iran war continues
articles.summary: Russian president warns world will suffer if Iran war continues Russian President Vladimir Putin said he hopes the conflict involving Iran will end “as soon as possible”, warning that the consequences of a prolonged war would be severe for the wider world. Putin said that if the fighting continues, “everyone would lose out”, as tensions in the Middle East continue to disrupt global energy markets and regional stability. Russia has sought to position itself as a diplomatic intermediary during the conflict while calling for negotiations and opposing further escalation in the region.
